quests.beneath.subtitle: Journey to the Center of the Earth
quests.tfg_tips.beneath.title: The Beneath
quests.tfg_tips.beneath.subtitle: Journey to the Center of the Earth
quests.tfg_tips.beneath.desc: Deep, DEEP underground, past the &dBedrock&r, you'll find &4The Beneath&r, a complex network of huge caves filled with dangers and strange flora and fauna. While &4The Beneath&r is completely optional, it contains a lot of resources as well as some quality of life improvements.
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_prep.title: Preparations
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_prep.subtitle: &4The Beneath&r is hostile, be prepared
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_prep.desc:
&4The Beneath&r is a very hostile environment. Due to it being very deep underground, the average temperature is always &cabove 20°C&r, going higher the deeper you go. This is not to mention the amount of hostile creatures crawling these caves.

The quests linked with this one provide some basic preparations for your adventure.
In &4The Beneath&r, with the temperature being relatively high, you should bring some &aWater&r or &aIce Soup&r to cool off on demand, or some &bPacked Ice&r (you can craft this out of ice shards and snow!) if you plan to make a temporary base.

Several kinds of drinks can also cool you off, such as &aLiquid Ice Slush&r.
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_clothes.title: Temperature Management: Passive
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_clothes.subtitle: Things to passively cool off
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_clothes.desc:
&bClothes&r also play an important role in cooling off. The easiest to craft before going to &4The Beneath&r are &dBurlap&r or &dLinen Clothes.&r

&dSilk Clothes&r require &aString&r, which can be found in large quantities inside the beneath biome called the &cWebbed Lair.&r

There is a better alternative if you have access to &9Blue Steel&r however...
quests.tfg_tips.rope_ladders.title: Rope Ladders
quests.tfg_tips.rope_ladders.subtitle: Going down!
quests.tfg_tips.rope_ladders.desc:
Rope Ladders are an advanced kind of ladder that are perfect for somewhere vertical like &4The Beneath&r.

Simply right-click on the edge of a cliff, and the rope ladder will automatically extend one block downwards for each rope ladder item you have in your inventory. Sneak-right-click the top of the rope ladder to pick it all back up at once.
quests.tfg_tips.gliders_and_grapples.des:
Pillaring up and bridging across gaps is so old fashioned. Get around quickly and efficiently with the &dHang Glider&r and &aGrappling Hook&r!

The &dHang Glider&r lets you travel horizontally long distances, which makes it great for crossing the wide open areas in &4The Beneath&r.

The &aGrappling Hook&r is fantastic for traveling in all directions, but requires a bit more care to use, so you don't accidentally miss your shot and go plummeting. It also comes with a huge variety of upgrades - check out the section in the &9Tips - Tools&r quest chapter for more details.
quests.tfg_tips.gliders_and_grapples.tas: Either a hang glider or a grappling hook
quests.tfg_tips.blue_steel_diving.title: Blue Steel Diving Gear
quests.tfg_tips.blue_steel_diving.subtit: Perfect for taking a warm lava bath
quests.tfg_tips.blue_steel_diving.desc: The &9Blue Steel Diving Gear&r is a special set of armor that makes you completely impervious to &cLava&r while you're wearing it. The reduced &aWarmth&r but high &bInsulation&r means you'll be able to keep a very stable body temperature while in &4The Beneath.&r
quests.tfg_tips.juicer.title: Juicer
quests.tfg_tips.juicer.subtitle: Hydration on a Pinch
quests.tfg_tips.juicer.desc: The &3Juicer&r is a special item that can extract the water contained in &aFruits&r, &aMushrooms&r, and &aCacti&r into drinkable &dWater&r.
quests.tfg_tips.piglin_disguise.subtitle: They'll never know...
quests.tfg_tips.piglin_disguise.desc:
Do you feel like you don't belong?
Do the &4Piglin&r attack you at your school because of your appearance?
Try wearing a &aPiglin Disguise&r to ward off unwanted attention and blend right in.
quests.tfg_tips.how_to_go_beneath.title: How to go to &4The Beneath&r
quests.tfg_tips.how_to_go_beneath.desc:
To reach &4The Beneath&r, you will first have to get through the layer of &aHornfels&r at the bottom of the world.

Once you've gotten past the Hornfels, you just have to &bstand on Bedrock&r, after which you will be &oteleported&r down under.

&3&lTip:&r&o You'll keep exactly the same X and Z coordinates, so it may teleport you inside some rock. It'll give you a 3x3x3 space to work with though, so be sure to bring some supports for tunneling your way out!
quests.tfg_tips.how_to_go_beneath.task.1: Reach and Stand on top of Bedrock
quests.tfg_tips.explosives.title: Entry #1: Explosives
quests.tfg_tips.explosives.desc:
One way to break through the Hornfels is with &dExplosives&r.

Powderkegs and Powderbarrels are your early-game options, while something like Dynamite and TNT can be useful later. You may also find Dynamite as loot.

This is the ideal option if you want to build an entrance to &4The Beneath&r right below your base.

Note that &aPrimitive Explosives&r are not strong enough to break Hornfels.
quests.tfg_tips.keratophyre.title: Entry #2: Find Keratophyre
quests.tfg_tips.keratophyre.desc:
Another way to break through the Hornfels is to let geology do it for you! Look for the more easily broken &2Keratophyre&r in caves, remnants of old geologic activity that penetrated this barrier.

You can use your minimap to spot it, as patches of a dark pink color.
quests.tfg_tips.steel_tools.title: Entry #3: Steel Tools
quests.tfg_tips.steel_tools.desc: Lastly, if you managed to source your Kaolin and Graphite solely through the overworld, you can just break the Hornfels with any tool made of Steel or a higher tier material.
quests.tfg_tips.steel_tools.task: A steel pickaxe or mining hammer
quests.tfg_tips.reach_the_beneath.title: Welcome to The Depths
quests.tfg_tips.reach_the_beneath.desc:
As soon as you reach &4The Beneath&r, you should make a &cwaypoint&r using your map so you don't lose your way back.

Unlike the vanilla Nether, there is no 8x distance multiplier, so don't try to use it for fast Overworld travel!

&4The Beneath&r is split into three layers. Each layer gets hotter and more dangerous as you descend.

The top layer is relatively safe and the temperature is easily manageable, so it's a good place to set up a little base of operations.
quests.tfg_tips.fuck_go_back.title: How to escape &4The Beneath&r
quests.tfg_tips.fuck_go_back.subtitle: Mom come pick me up, I'm scared.
quests.tfg_tips.fuck_go_back.desc: To escape &4The Beneath&r, you need to reach the upper levels of the cave system, near the &dBedrock Ceiling&r. Reaching the Bedrock here will transport you back to the &bOverworld&r, right where you stood on to get in.
quests.tfg_tips.piglin_bartering.title: Piglin Bartering
quests.tfg_tips.piglin_bartering.desc:
The curious inhabitants of &4The Beneath&r have items to offer... Trade gold with them to get some useful resource, such as small amounts of &aMetals&r or &aLeather.&r
Check out the &2Field Guide&r for details about barter trades.
quests.tfg_tips.hellforge.title: The Hellforge
quests.tfg_tips.hellforge.subtitle: Super-size me!
quests.tfg_tips.hellforge.desc:
The &cHellforge&r is a gratuitously large version of a Charcoal Forge. It's entirely optional, but it comes with these benefits:

- A bigger inventory for fuel and items to melt
- Doesn't require sky access, so it works in &4The Beneath&r
- Can heat up to 9 crucibles at a time

Check the Field Guide for instructions on how to build the multiblock structure.
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_tower.title: Piglin Towers
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_tower.subtitle: They've learnt civil engineering
quests.tfg_tips.beneath_tower.desc:
Need another way to get down to the lower levels of &4The Beneath&r, or searching for a way to get back up?

The local residents have constructed tall spiral staircase structures precisely for this reason, although they may not take kindly to &aoutsiders&r using it...
quests.tfg_tips.unposter.subtitle: Essential for every Fun Guy
quests.tfg_tips.unposter.desc:
The &cUnposter&r is used to grow mushrooms.

You use it similarly to a Composter, except instead of making compost, when a mushroom is within the Unposter's 11x11 range, it will consume its deposited items to multiply that mushroom in the area around it.

Note that it only works on mushrooms you find naturally growing in the world. Once you pick a mushroom up, you can't replant it.
quests.tfg_tips.top_slimes.title: Tameable Slimes
quests.tfg_tips.top_slimes.subtitle: Isn't this just Slime Rancher?
quests.tfg_tips.top_slimes.desc.1:
In &4The Beneath&r you'll find the usual aggressive green slimes you're used to, but you might also find some new &efriendlier slimes&r in certain environments.

These can be tamed similar to animals on the surface, by feeding them &6mushrooms&r to raise their familiarity. Slimes are hermaphoditic, meaning any slime can breed with another of its same type. Each slime also has its own uses - some are used for producing items, while others provide more passive effects.

Raise your slime's familiarity enough and they can also become your pets, letting you issue commands to them.
quests.tfg_tips.top_slimes.desc.2:
On the top layer, you can encounter &bIce Slimes&r and &aPlant Slimes&r.

- &bIce Slimes&r provide a cooling effect to the player when nearby, but do not produce anything.

- &aPlant Slimes&r produce Plant Slime Balls which can be crushed into Plant Balls for fertiliser.

Note that unlike surface animals, slimes &lonly&r provide resources &dwhen a player feeds them&r, and &lonly&r when they're in &4The Beneath&r.
quests.tfg_tips.middle_beneath.title: The Second Layer
quests.tfg_tips.middle_beneath.subtitle: Dark, darker, yet darker
quests.tfg_tips.middle_beneath.desc:
The middle layer of &4The Beneath&r is where the heat starts to become an actual threat. However, this is also where you'll start finding more of the valuable resources, such as &dKaolin Clay&r.

Make sure you have ample preparations before descending!
quests.tfg_tips.red_elk.title: Red Elk
quests.tfg_tips.red_elk.desc: &cRed Elk&r are one of the local wild animals in &4The Beneath&r. They can only be found on the middle and bottom layers, and are your only source of &cLavaproof Hides&r. You can also use these hides for other leather purposes.
quests.tfg_tips.middle_slimes.title: More Tameable Slimes
quests.tfg_tips.middle_slimes.subtitle: Super adaptable!
quests.tfg_tips.middle_slimes.desc:
The middle layer is home to some new kinds of slimes:

- &6Glowberry Slimes&r can be found in the Lush Hollow biome, and produce a kind of slime that can be turned into &eSugar&r.

- &3Spring Slimes&r are residents of the Geothermal Springs biome, and currently do nothing but may be useful in the future.

- &aPlant Slimes&r also live in the Thorny Grove biome.
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_beneath.title: The Third Layer
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_beneath.subtitle: The Capital of the Unreturned
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_beneath.desc:
It's really hot! You'll only be able to stay here for short periods of time until you're able to craft things like &bBlue Ice&r or the &9Blue Steel Diving Gear&r. Full temperature immunity won't exist until &6HV&r...

The bottom of &4The Beneath&r is home to an excess amount of ores and guaranteed graphite, but of course it's also the most dangerous.
quests.tfg_tips.bastions.title: Bastions
quests.tfg_tips.bastions.subtitle: They've seen better days...
quests.tfg_tips.bastions.desc:
&cBastions&r can be found in the bottom layer of &4The Beneath&r.

They're heavily fortified and guarded, but also full of tasty loot. Raid one if you're up for a challenge!
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_slimes.title: Even More Tameable Slimes
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_slimes.subtitle: Exotic varieties!
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_slimes.desc.1:
Besides Magma Cubes, only one kind of friendly slime can survive in the bottom layer of &4The Beneath&r: &cLava Slimes&r, which provide a heating effect to the player when nearby.

This by itself sounds pretty useless, but Lava Slimes have a secret: they can be crossbred with &aPlant Slimes&r to produce &eLatex Slimes&r, which can be harvested for Latex Slime Balls for all your rubber needs.

Reminder that unlike surface animals, slimes &lonly&r provide resources &dwhen a player feeds them&r, and &lonly&r when they're in &4The Beneath&r.
quests.tfg_tips.bottom_slimes.desc.2:
Providing ambient heat is pretty redundant for a place that's already trying to cook you alive, but if you live in a cold climate, you can take one of these back up to the surface by &eSneak-Right Clicking&r while both of your hands are empty to pick them up, and then visit the Bedrock ceiling as usual.

Lava Slimes can appear in any of the biomes on the bottom layer.
